one_time_description: Land and building contributed by Kahkewistahaw 1907 Specific Claim Trust ($6,543,506); prior year included Mismanagement Claim Settlement of $49,403,870 and related distributions
economic_notes: First Nation operates Kahkewistahaw Birds Point Resort Limited Partnership and Weyotisiwin Holdings Limited Partnership (consolidated). GBEs include Kahkewistahaw Development LP, Kahkewistahaw Management LP, Kihew Fab-Co LP. Penipa Hotel LP (100% owned by Kahkewistahaw Development LP) has Affinity Credit Union loan of $3,304,420. First Nation initiated flood claim against Government of Canada. CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative funding received ($2,065,281). COVID-19 impacted operations including office closures and travel restrictions.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Province is Saskatchewan (SK). Audit emphasis of matter regarding flood claim against Government of Canada with associated insurance loan. Prior year (2020) included large one-time Mismanagement Claim Settlement of $49,403,870 with offsetting Legacy Trust contribution ($35M), legal fees ($2.47M), and per capita distribution ($4.097M). Kahkewistahaw 1907 Specific Claim Trust - Trust Account and Kahkewistahaw Legacy Trust are excluded from consolidation as not controlled by Chief and Council. Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Net debt position of $26.2M. ISC funding calculated as ISC ($12,439,571) + ISC-FNIHB ($1,535,234) + ISC-FNIHB Home Care ($179,347) = $14,153,152. Total government transfers include ISC, FNIHB, CMHC, and YTC Child & Family Services. OSR includes land lease, grants in lieu, FNGST fee, restaurant sales, interest, member service fee, trust payments, etc.
